mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which translates into stable corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, low unemployment correlates with economic stability.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of periods where UNRATE is below its 10-year average, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer spending and demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS 3-month percentage change and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) during periods of economic expansion, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A stable and low inflation environment reduces uncertainty for businesses and consumers, supports purchasing power, and allows for lower discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Low inflation typically supports higher equity valuations.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when CPIAUCSL 12-month percentage change is below 3%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a rapidly improving labor market, leading to increased household income and consumption, which positively impacts corporate earnings and equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is a key indicator of economic recovery.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation (p > 0.1) with PAYEMS 1-month percentage change during post-recession recoveries, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and an increase in the unemployment rate signals a severe contraction in both construction activity and overall labor market health, indicating an impending economic downturn that will negatively impact corporate profits and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and labor markets are leading indicators of recession.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 30% of instances where HOUST falls below 500k units (3-month average) AND UNRATE rises above 7.0% (3-month average), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate by the central bank indicates a tightening monetary policy, which raises the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, thereby dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Higher interest rates increase borrowing costs.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during periods where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% over three months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in industrial production reflects strong manufacturing and mining activity, indicating a healthy supply side of the economy and strong demand for goods, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key measure of economic output.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive relationship (p > 0.1) with INDPRO 6-month percentage change during periods of industrial expansion, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and robust retail sales indicates a powerful and broad-based economic expansion, signaling strong corporate fundamentals and consumer confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP and retail sales are primary indicators of economic health.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when both GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth exceeds 2.5% and RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds 0.5%, the hypothesis is rejected.
